title: Caryococca dependens

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, and is believed to offer potential health benefits in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Caryococca dependens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
